subject: PLEASE HELP - Cannot access SharePoint site for the quarterly report that is due by end of week and my manager Sarah Thompson from the Wealth Management division specifically the portfolio analytics sub-team has been asking me about it every single day for the past three days
description:
Hi IT Support Team,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to you today because I have been experiencing a very frustrating issue with accessing our SharePoint site. I know you are all very busy and I really appreciate everything you do for us, but this is becoming quite urgent.

So basically what happened is that last Monday (or was it Tuesday? I think it was Monday) I tried to log into the SharePoint site where we keep all of our quarterly financial reports. You know the one - it's the site that has all the Excel spreadsheets and PowerPoint presentations for the wealth management team. Anyway, when I tried to access it, I got an error message. I don't remember exactly what it said but it was something about permissions or access denied or something like that.

I tried again on Tuesday and the same thing happened. Then on Wednesday I asked my colleague Jennifer (jennifer.wu@contoso.com) if she could access it and she said she could access it just fine, so I think it's just my account that has the problem.

I should mention that I recently changed teams - I moved from the Retail Banking division to the Wealth Management division about two weeks ago. Before the transfer I could access all the SharePoint sites I needed without any problems. After the transfer, I noticed that some of my access seemed to change. I can still access the main company intranet and the HR portal and Outlook and Teams all work fine. It's just this one SharePoint site.

I tried a few things to fix it myself:
1. I cleared my browser cache and cookies (I use Chrome)
2. I tried using Edge instead of Chrome
3. I tried using an InPrivate/Incognito window
4. I restarted my computer
5. I even tried accessing it from my phone using the SharePoint mobile app

None of these things worked. I still get the same error every time.

The reason this is urgent is because we have a quarterly report due by end of day Friday March 21st. The report template and all the source data files are stored on this SharePoint site. Without access, I literally cannot do my job this week. My manager Sarah Thompson has been asking me about the status of the report every day and I keep having to tell her I'm waiting on IT to fix my access.

I know this might be related to my department transfer but I'm not sure who to talk to about getting my permissions updated. Our team admin said she put in a request but nothing has happened yet.

Also, while I have your attention, I have a couple of other minor things:
- My OneDrive is showing that I'm almost at my storage limit (it says 95% full). Is there any way to get more storage?
- The printer on the 2nd floor (the big one near the coffee machine) has been jamming a lot lately. I don't know if that's something you handle or if it's facilities.

But the SharePoint access is the main urgent thing.

Please let me know if you need any more information from me. My extension is 4829 and I'm usually at my desk between 8am and 6pm. You can also reach me on Teams.

Thank you so much for your help!

Best regards,
Amanda Foster
Senior Financial Analyst
Wealth Management Division
Extension: 4829
Building 3, Floor 2, Desk 2-47

P.S. I forgot to mention - I also can't access the team's shared OneNote notebook that's linked to the same SharePoint site. Not sure if that's related.

P.P.S. Jennifer said she thinks it might be because my account hasn't been fully migrated to the new team's security group. Does that sound right?

attachments.subject: URGENT - Printer not working & need help ASAP!!!
attachments.description:
Hi IT Team,

The printer on Floor 5 (HP LaserJet Pro M404n) is showing an "Paper Jam" error but there's no paper jammed anywhere. I've checked all the trays and the back panel.

I've tried:

  • Power cycling the printer
  • Removing and reinserting all paper trays
  • Clearing the print queue

Nothing works. I have 200 pages of compliance reports that need to be printed and distributed by 3 PM today for the board meeting.


Thanks,
Amanda Torres

Contoso Financial Services

reporter.subject: Database connection timeout
reporter.description:
SELECT * FROM production.client_accounts WHERE status = 'active' AND last_login < '2026-01-01' ORDER BY account_value DESC;

-- Above query has been timing out for the past 3 hours on the prod SQL Server instance (sql-prod-east.contoso.internal). Normally runs in ~2 seconds. Connection string: Server=sql-prod-east.contoso.internal;Database=ClientDB;Trusted_Connection=True;Connection Timeout=30;

I checked sys.dm_exec_requests and there are 47 blocked sessions. Looks like there might be a long-running transaction holding locks. The blocking_session_id points to SPID 89 which has been running for 6+ hours.

This is affecting the client portal — customers can't view their account balances. ~500 users impacted based on the error rate in Application Insights.

Resource group: rg-data-prod-east
Server: sql-prod-east (Azure SQL Managed Instance, Business Critical tier)
Database: ClientDB

reporter.subject: Network issue — see attached log (300MB)
reporter.description:
Our entire floor (Building 2, Floor 7) has been experiencing intermittent network drops every 15-20 minutes since Monday. I've captured packet traces and switch logs. The drops last about 30-60 seconds each time and affect both wired and wireless connections.

I noticed the issue correlates with spikes in broadcast traffic from what looks like a misconfigured device at MAC address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF. The ARP table shows this device flip-flopping between two IP addresses.

Attaching everything I've gathered — sorry about the file sizes, the pcap alone is quite large.

4) Oh and FYI — the coffee machine in the 2nd floor kitchen is broken again. I know that's not IT but I don't know who else to tell.

5) One more thing — my colleague Sarah needs access to the same SharePoint site I requested last month. Can you add sarah.chen@contoso.com to the Finance Reports site? She just transferred to our team.

Thanks,
Michael

reporter.subject: Fwd: Fwd: Fwd: Fwd: Fwd: Weekly IT Newsletter - but also my laptop is slow
reporter.description:
---------- Forwarded message (5 of 5) ----------
From: IT Communications <it-comms@contoso.com>
Date: March 10, 2026
Subject: Weekly IT Newsletter - March 10

=== CONTOSO IT WEEKLY UPDATE ===

Planned Maintenance:
- March 14: SAP system upgrade (2am-6am EST)
- March 15: Network switch replacement Building 3 (midnight-4am)
- March 16: Email archive migration (no downtime expected)

Tips & Tricks:
- Did you know you can use Ctrl+Shift+T to reopen closed browser tabs?
- New self-service password reset portal: https://passwordreset.contoso.com
- Reminder: All USB drives must be encrypted per policy SEC-2024-003

Upcoming Training:
- March 20: Cybersecurity Awareness (mandatory)
- March 25: Advanced Excel for Finance Teams
- March 28: Azure DevOps for Beginners

Contact us: helpdesk@contoso.com | ext. 1000 | Building 1, Floor 1
=== END NEWSLETTER ===

---------- End forwarded messages ----------

Sorry for the long forward chain. But actually I wanted to ask - my laptop has been really slow since the SAP upgrade mentioned in this newsletter (March 14). Everything takes forever to load, especially Outlook and Teams. Restarted multiple times, no improvement. Dell Latitude 5540, Windows 11. Is the SAP upgrade related? It started right after.
